perItemFindings.label: lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl
perItemFindings.one_line: The plan rebuilds a Bernoulli KL band bound after only searching Stat/Minimax, but Causalean already provides `Causalean.Mathlib.Analysis.bernoulli_kl_le_four_sq_sub_of_mem_quarter_band` and `Causalean.Mathlib.Probability.bernoulliLaw_klDiv_le_four_sq_sub`; the node only needs an affine {-B,B} mean-channel wrapper.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan `lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl` to reuse the existing Causalean Bernoulli KL band theorem rather than proving the scalar KL inequality from scratch. A thin local wrapper may still be needed to translate p=(1+u/B)/2 and q=(1+v/B)/2 and push the {0,1} Bernoulli law to {-B,B}, but the substantive KL estimate should be recorded as reuse of `bernoulliLaw_klDiv_le_four_sq_sub` / `bernoulli_kl_le_four_sq_sub_of_mem_quarter_band`. The other define-local and deferred decisions checked are sound partial-fit/bypass choices.

perItemFindings.label: lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl
perItemFindings.one_line: The cited decl Causalean.Mathlib.Probability.bernoulliLaw_klDiv_le_four_sq_sub states an ENNReal KL bound for bernoulliLaw p and bernoulliLaw q on the {0,1} Bernoulli encoding with p,q in [1/4,3/4]; the core node needs a KL bound for arbitrary mean-parameterized laws Q_u,Q_v on {-B,B} with hypotheses B>0 and |u|,|v|<=B/2.

review.verbatim_critique: Re-plan lem:bernoulli-mean-channel-kl as define-local, not one-to-one reuse. The Bernoulli band theorem is the right substrate and is not sorry-backed, but it does not itself realize the node's signature or conclusion: F3 must build the affine {-B,B} mean-channel wrapper, prove/transport the KL identity or invariance between Q_u/Q_v and bernoulliLaw ((1+u/B)/2)/((1+v/B)/2), discharge the band hypotheses, and simplify the constant. Keep Causalean.Mathlib.Probability.bernoulliLaw_klDiv_le_four_sq_sub in notes as an internal reused ingredient rather than the node's reuse discharge.
